item.title: I'm Just a Bill: National Flood Insurance Program Reform
item.description:
👀 NFIP Reform

What

National Flood Insurance Program reauthorization + reform in the 119th Congress.

What it does

  • Actuarial-soundness vs. affordability balance · Risk Rating 2.0 modifications · cooperative-class housing-trust eligibility · Tribal coastal provisions.

Composes

Climate Disaster Resilience + Save-the-World Paper 3 (Universal Cooperative Shelter).

🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: National Popular Vote Interstate Compact Resolution
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — National Popular Vote Interstate Compact Resolution

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Congressional resolutions in the 119th Congress addressing the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact (NPV) — an agreement among states to award their electoral votes to the national popular-vote winner. The compact has been ratified by states totaling approximately 209 electoral votes (61 short of 270 majority).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Native American Self-Determination Act Amendments
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Native American Self-Determination Act Amendments

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Amendments to the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (ISDEAA) in the 119th Congress, expanding Tribal authority to administer federal programs and addressing the documented Tribal-sovereignty gaps in federal funding administration.

What does it actually do?

  • 638 contracts expansion — broadens federal programs eligible for direct Tribal administration
  • Indirect cost coverage — full federal-coverage of indirect costs for 638-contracted programs
  • Fee-to-trust process — streamlines land-into-trust applications
  • Tribal climate adaptation — funding stream for Tribal climate-adaptation projects
  • Public Law 280 states — addresses jurisdictional ambiguity in PL280 states

Who votes when?

  • House Natural Resources + Senate Indian Affairs active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with Save-the-World Paper 5 (Universal Earth Stewardship — Tribal sovereignty as cooperative-class partnership), Save-the-World Paper 7 (Health Care — Indian Health Service modernization composing), and Save-the-World Paper 9 (Universal Education — Tribal college and university funding).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Older Americans Act Reauthorization
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Older Americans Act Reauthorization

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Reauthorization of the Older Americans Act (OAA) in the 119th Congress. The OAA funds Area Agencies on Aging, congregate and home-delivered meals (Meals on Wheels), in-home services, family caregiver support, and elder-rights services.

What does it actually do?

  • Reauthorizes federal grant programs supporting older-adult community services through 2030.
  • Updates the program structure to address gaps identified since the 2020 reauthorization.
  • Caregiver support: Expands National Family Caregiver Support Program funding.
  • Nutrition services: Modifies congregate-meal program structure to accommodate post-2020 service-delivery patterns.
  • Elder-rights: Strengthens elder-abuse prevention and reporting infrastructure.

Who votes when?

  • House Education and Workforce + Senate HELP active consideration; reauthorization typically bipartisan.

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes directly with #5 The Family Table ($11T unpaid-care-work + Ashton Applewhite Age Champion) and #6 Tatiana Schlossberg Health Accords. Federal OAA funding is the safety-net floor; cooperative Family Table Trust Graph + MSA emergency-pool is the structural complement.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (H.R.1, 119th Congress)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — H.R.1 “One Big Beautiful Bill Act”

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

H.R.1 is the comprehensive reconciliation package moving through the 119th Congress in 2025-26. It bundles tax provisions, social safety net changes, and federal spending priorities into a single budget reconciliation vehicle.

“Reconciliation” means it only needs a simple majority in the Senate (51 votes), not the usual 60-vote filibuster threshold. That’s why so much gets packed into one bill.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Opioid Settlement Funds Protection Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Opioid Settlement Funds Protection

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Federal protection legislation in the 119th Congress for the ~$50B in opioid-settlement funds flowing to state and local governments. Addresses the documented diversion of opioid-settlement funds to general-revenue purposes rather than substance-use-disorder treatment and recovery.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal accountability framework for state opioid-settlement spending
  • Treatment-and-recovery-services minimum allocation requirements
  • Transparency reporting requirements for state opioid funds
  • Federal grant matching for jurisdictions exceeding minimum allocation thresholds
  • Indigenous-community-impact provisions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #5 The Family Table (recovery and care intersection), #7 MSA (Substitution-routing for participating recovery providers), and #8 Defense Klaus (overlap with survivor-class infrastructure when SUD intersects domestic violence). Composes with Rural Mental Health Access Act Bounty Poster.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Overdraft Fee Cap Legislation
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Overdraft Fee Cap

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Federal overdraft-fee cap legislation in the 119th Congress. Current bank overdraft fees average $30-35; documented extraction pattern from low-income account holders.

What does it actually do?

  • Fee cap — limits per-overdraft fee (typically proposed $3-$10)
  • Per-day cap — limits aggregate daily overdraft fees
  • Reasonable-and-proportional standard — requires fees be tied to actual costs
  • Disclosure — strengthens overdraft-protection disclosure requirements
  • Credit-union exemption — most proposals carve out small credit unions

Who votes when?

  • House Financial Services + Senate Banking active consideration; CFPB rulemaking parallel track

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #7 MSA (Cathie Mahon, Inclusiv credit-union model) and #10 VSL peer-to-peer cooperative finance. Cooperative-class members who currently absorb overdraft fees would benefit; the cooperative-finance trio is the structural alternative to bank-class fee-extraction.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Patent Troll Reform
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Patent Troll Reform

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Patent troll (NPE) reform legislation in the 119th Congress addressing abusive patent-litigation patterns from non-practicing entities.

What does it actually do?

  • Heightened pleading standards for patent infringement claims
  • Fee-shifting in frivolous-claim outcomes
  • Customer-stay provisions
  • IPR (Inter Partes Review) procedural reform

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with The 300 patent-pledge cascade (cooperative’s defensive-only patent posture). Patent troll reform composes with the cooperative’s structural commitment to never use patents against people building something good.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Pharmaceutical Antitrust Enforcement
item.description:
👀 Pharmaceutical Antitrust

Pharmaceutical antitrust enforcement 119th Congress: pay-for-delay enforcement · patent-thicket reform · biologic-biosimilar competition. Composes with Antitrust Merger Review + FDA Modernization + Medicare Drug Negotiation Expansion. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Post-Loper-Bright Agency Authority Restoration
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Post-Loper-Bright Agency Authority

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Legislation in the 119th Congress responding to the 2024 Supreme Court Loper Bright decision overturning Chevron deference. Various proposals reaffirm agency interpretive authority by statute on a case-by-case basis.

What does it actually do?

  • Statutory delegation of interpretive authority for specific agencies/topics
  • Sector-specific (environmental, healthcare, financial-services, food-safety) variations
  • Court-of-jurisdiction provisions for agency-action review
  • Rulemaking-process modifications

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Many cooperative-class composing federal-policy floors operate via agency rulemaking. Post-Chevron uncertainty affects nearly every Bounty Poster on this list. The cooperative does not endorse positions on this constitutional/administrative-law question; the Council exists to provide accurate plain-language explanation.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Postal Service Reform Extensions
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Postal Service Reform Extensions

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Continued reform of the U.S. Postal Service in the 119th Congress, building on the 2022 Postal Service Reform Act. Provisions address service quality, financial sustainability, and rural-area delivery integrity.

What does it actually do?

  • Service standards: Codifies minimum delivery standards, especially in rural and last-mile areas.
  • Financial reform: Continues prefunding-relief; adjusts pension and benefits accounting.
  • Postal Banking pilot: Expands USPS pilot program for basic financial services in postal facilities.
  • Vehicle electrification: Funds USPS fleet transition timeline.

Who votes when?

  • House Oversight + Senate HSGAC active consideration.

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

USPS rural delivery is critical infrastructure for #1 Lets Make Dinner Freezer Host distribution and #2 Lets Get Groceries local-supply-chain logistics. Postal Banking pilot composes with #7 MSA member financial-services architecture for the unbanked.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Pregnant Workers Fairness Act Enforcement
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Pregnant Workers Fairness Act Enforcement

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

The Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (PWFA) was signed in 2022 and went into effect June 27, 2023. The 119th Congress is considering enforcement-strengthening provisions and addressing implementation challenges identified in the EEOC final regulations.

What does it actually do?

  • Reasonable accommodations: Requires covered employers (15+ employees) to provide reasonable accommodations for known limitations related to pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions.
  • EEOC enforcement: Strengthens EEOC investigation and enforcement authority.
  • Private right of action: Workers can file suit; remedies parallel Title VII.
  • Coverage: Applies before, during, and after pregnancy.
  • Documentation: Limits what employers can require for accommodation requests.

Who votes when?

  • House and Senate: Active enforcement-amendment consideration
  • EEOC regulations: Already finalized and in effect
  • President: Signature dependent on amendment scope

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #5 The Family Table (Ai-jen Poo + Ashton Applewhite + Marc Freedman three-Crown architecture) and the cooperative’s broader $11 trillion unpaid-care-work framing. PWFA enforcement directly affects the working-mother cooperative-class member experience — and the cooperative’s Initiative #5 is structurally designed to fill the gaps PWFA leaves (informal caregivers, unpaid care work, intergenerational care coordination).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Presidential Pardon Process Reform
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Presidential Pardon Process Reform

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Presidential pardon process reform in the 119th Congress addressing transparency around pardons, advisory-committee structure, and post-pardon disclosure.

What does it actually do?

  • Pardon Office staffing + capacity requirements
  • Public disclosure of pardon-recipient applications
  • Advisory committee structure
  • Crime-victim notification provisions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

The cooperative does not endorse positions on this constitutional/executive-power question. The Council exists to provide accurate plain-language explanation. Composes with Save-the-World Paper 10 (Universal Justice — Federal-Congressional Body Cam doctrine).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: PRESS Act (Federal Journalist Shield Law)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — PRESS Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

The Protect Reporters from Exploitative State Spying (PRESS) Act in the 119th Congress establishing a federal shield law for journalists protecting source confidentiality, with specified exceptions.

What does it actually do?

  • Source protection — federal protection for journalist-source confidentiality
  • Exceptions — clear exceptions for terrorism, kidnapping, imminent harm
  • Definition of “journalist” — broad enough to cover independent and cooperative-class journalists, narrow enough to exclude bad-faith actors
  • Tech-company protection — limits federal subpoenas to tech companies seeking journalist communications

Who votes when?

  • House Judiciary + Senate Judiciary active consideration; historically bipartisan with intelligence-community concerns

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #9 Rally Group member-organized civic-information work, #15 Political Expedition Council “I’m Just a Bill” pilot vetting infrastructure, and the cooperative’s broader Federal-Congressional Body Cam transparency doctrine.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Public Banking Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Public Banking Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Public Banking enabling legislation in the 119th Congress. Builds on the existing Bank of North Dakota model and recent state-level public-banking laws (California, New Mexico). Federal version creates regulatory framework for state and municipal public banks.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal regulatory clarity for state/municipal public banks
  • FDIC insurance pathway adapted for public-banking entity class
  • Federal Reserve account access for qualified public banks
  • Municipal-bond financing facilitation
  • Tribal Public Banking provisions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #7 MSA (Cathie Mahon credit-union model — public banks compose with credit unions in the cooperative-finance ecosystem) and broader cooperative-class financial infrastructure. Public banks are the structural alternative to bank-class extraction at the municipal scale.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Public Domain Restoration
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Public Domain Restoration

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Copyright reform in the 119th Congress addressing the documented public-domain shortage created by repeated copyright-term extensions (Sonny Bono Act, Mickey Mouse Protection Act variants).

What does it actually do?

  • Tightens orphan-works treatment
  • Renewal-registration option for post-1978 works (formerly required)
  • Library/archive fair-use clarification
  • Statutory-damages cap modifications
  • Anti-circumvention DMCA Section 1201 review

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #13 JukeBox (cooperative music licensing depends on understanding what’s in the public domain), Save Local Journalism Act (fair-use for archival journalism), and #12 Harper Guild + #14 Didasko (cooperative-education materials benefit from larger public domain).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Public Service Loan Forgiveness Reform (S.4350)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Public Service Loan Forgiveness Reform (S.4350)

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

S.4350 in the 119th Congress would clarify and expand Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) eligibility, particularly for cooperative-class workers and certain nonprofit-sector employees whose eligibility has been historically ambiguous.

What does it actually do?

  • Eligibility clarification: Expands the definition of “qualifying employer” to include certain cooperative-sector and worker-owned enterprises.
  • Payment counting: Allows previously-disqualified payments under prior repayment plans to count toward the 120-payment threshold.
  • Forbearance correction: Addresses the 2023 PSLF guidance issues for borrowers placed in incorrect forbearance.
  • Servicer accountability: Strengthens oversight of PSLF servicers to prevent improper denials.

Who votes when?

  • Senate HELP Committee: Active consideration
  • House companion: Pending introduction
  • President: Signature expected if both chambers pass

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Directly affects the cooperative’s labor-class membership pool. Cooperative-employer eligibility for PSLF is a structural recognition that worker-owned enterprises are part of the public-good economy. Composes with:

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Public Transit Frequency Standards
item.description:
👀 Public Transit Frequency

Federal transit frequency standards 119th Congress: minimum service-frequency for federally-funded transit · low-income corridor priority. Composes with Federal Bus System + BEAD Broadband + Save-the-World Paper 3. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rapid Rehousing Funding Expansion
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Rapid Rehousing Funding

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Rapid Rehousing funding expansion in the 119th Congress, addressing the documented shortage of rapid-rehousing capacity in nearly every metropolitan Continuum of Care (CoC).

What does it actually do?

  • HUD Continuum of Care rapid-rehousing funding expansion
  • Move-in assistance + short-to-medium-term rental subsidy
  • Case-management funding for rehousing transitions
  • Family-with-children prioritization
  • Veterans rapid-rehousing parallel funding stream

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #1 Lets Make Dinner Homeless Help Solution (cooperative-class composing layer for federal homelessness response), Save-the-World Paper 3 (Universal Cooperative Shelter), and #5 The Family Table Trust Graph for rehousing-recipient family-network reconnection.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Renewable Energy PTC/ITC Continuation
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Renewable Energy PTC/ITC Continuation

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Continuation of the federal Production Tax Credit (PTC) and Investment Tax Credit (ITC) for renewable energy in the 119th Congress. PTC/ITC were extended by the 2022 IRA; current legislation addresses ongoing structure and qualifying technology definitions.

What does it actually do?

  • PTC continuation — extends per-kWh production credit for wind, solar, geothermal, hydro
  • ITC continuation — extends investment tax credit for solar, storage, fuel cells
  • Domestic content — bonus credit for projects meeting domestic-content thresholds
  • Energy communities — bonus credit for projects in fossil-fuel-transition communities
  • Community-owned generation — provisions specifically enabling cooperative-class member-owned generation

Who votes when?

  • House Ways & Means + Senate Finance active consideration; closely watched by the energy sector

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Direct composing with Save-the-World Paper 2 (Universal Abundant Low-Cost Energy). Community-owned generation provisions enable cooperative-class member-pooled solar/storage installations that share the federal tax-credit incentive structure.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Right to Repair (H.R.906)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Right to Repair (H.R.906)

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

H.R.906 in the 119th Congress would establish federal Right-to-Repair protections covering consumer electronics, agricultural equipment, and automotive systems. State-level Right-to-Repair laws have passed in multiple states; this bill would set a federal floor.

What does it actually do?

  • Manufacturer obligations: Manufacturers must make repair documentation, parts, and software tools available to independent repair shops and end-users.
  • Anti-circumvention: Prohibits manufacturers from using software locks to prevent third-party repair.
  • Agricultural equipment: Specifically addresses tractor and farm equipment lockouts that have been a flashpoint for farmers.
  • Consumer electronics: Phones, laptops, appliances covered.
  • Enforcement: FTC authority to enforce; private right of action included.

Who votes when?

  • House Energy & Commerce + Judiciary: Active consideration
  • Senate companion: Pending
  • President: Signature dependent on chamber action

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes directly with two cooperative initiatives:

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Attorney Recruitment Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Rural Attorney Recruitment

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Rural Attorney Recruitment in the 119th Congress addressing documented “legal deserts” — rural counties with declining attorney populations and limited access to legal services.

What does it actually do?

  • Loan-forgiveness for attorneys serving designated rural counties
  • Federal grants for state-bar rural-attorney programs
  • Tribal Attorney recruitment provisions
  • Rural-court technology infrastructure
  • Composes with State Bar Open Letter framework

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with LBCCL Healthcare Galveston Legal Framework first-deployment + future LBCCL variant deployments in rural jurisdictions where attorney shortages limit cooperative-class legal-services accessibility.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Electric Cooperative Broadband Authority
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Rural Electric Cooperative Broadband Authority

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Federal authorization in the 119th Congress for rural electric cooperatives to expand into broadband service delivery, leveraging existing fiber-and-pole infrastructure.

What does it actually do?

  • USDA Rural Utilities Service loan eligibility for cooperative broadband
  • BEAD program coordination provisions
  • Cooperative-cross-subsidy clarity
  • Federal-pole-attachment authority for cooperative broadband providers

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with BEAD Broadband Expansion + Universal Broadband Floor + Federal Broadband Affordability Bounty Posters. Direct kin-cooperative federation potential — rural electric cooperatives expanding into broadband are natural cooperative-class peers.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Electrification 2.0 Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Rural Electrification 2.0

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Rural electric cooperative modernization legislation in the 119th Congress, addressing aging rural-electric infrastructure, clean-energy transition financing, and rural-cooperative debt-restructuring options.

What does it actually do?

  • USDA Rural Utilities Service loan modernization
  • Cooperative-utility clean-energy transition financing
  • Rural-broadband + rural-electric infrastructure-coordination provisions
  • Rural-cooperative governance modernization (Crown candidate references abound)

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Direct composing structurally — rural electric cooperatives are the legacy cooperative-class infrastructure (~1,000 cooperatives serving ~42 million Americans). Their modernization composes with Liana Banyan’s cooperative-class architecture as kin-cooperative federation potential.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Emergency Services Funding
item.description:
👀 Rural Emergency Services

Rural EMS funding 119th Congress: volunteer EMS provider provisions · ambulance reimbursement reform · rural-EMS workforce. Composes with Rural Hospital Stabilization + Rural Mental Health + Federal Bus System. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Grocery Tax Credit
item.description:
👀 Rural Grocery Tax Credit

What

Federal rural grocery tax credit in the 119th Congress addressing food-desert formation in rural communities (~30M Americans live more than 10 miles from a grocery store).

What it does

  • Tax credit for grocery operators in designated rural food deserts · cooperative-grocery model preference · USDA technical-assistance integration.

Composes

#2 Lets Get Groceries (Stacy Mitchell ILSR model directly) · Farm Bill Reauthorization.

🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Hospital Stabilization Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Rural Hospital Stabilization

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Rural hospital stabilization legislation in the 119th Congress addressing the documented closure rate of rural hospitals (~150+ closed since 2010, with hundreds more financially at-risk).

What does it actually do?

  • Enhanced Medicare reimbursement for designated rural hospitals
  • Critical Access Hospital program modernization
  • Rural Emergency Hospital designation expansion
  • Telehealth permanence for rural-hospital service-line continuity
  • Workforce provisions composing with rural mental health legislation

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #6 Tatiana Schlossberg Health Accords + #7 MSA. Rural members face the documented care-desert created by hospital closures. The cooperative composes with whatever federal floor exists.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Mental Health Access Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Rural Mental Health Access Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Mental health access legislation in the 119th Congress targeting rural and frontier communities where the documented mental-health-provider shortage is most severe.

What does it actually do?

  • Telehealth expansion — extends rural telehealth reimbursement parity
  • Provider recruitment — National Health Service Corps loan-repayment expansion for rural mental-health providers
  • Crisis services — funds rural 988 + Veterans Crisis Line capacity
  • Community Health Workers — funding for non-clinical peer-support roles in rural communities
  • Loan repayment — student-loan forgiveness for mental-health providers serving rural designations

Who votes when?

  • Senate HELP + House Energy and Commerce active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #5 The Family Table rural family-care infrastructure, #6 Tatiana Schlossberg Health Accords (rural Galveston-pattern composition), and #8 Defense Klaus rural survivor-class crisis-response.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Rural Postal Service Protection
item.description:
👀 Rural Postal Service

Rural postal service protection 119th Congress: rural delivery standard preservation · POStPlan facility reversal · rural-route mail-carrier provisions. Composes with Postal Service Reform + rural infrastructure cluster. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: SAVE Act (H.R.22, 119th Congress)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — SAVE Act (H.R.22)

Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

The SAVE Act would require documentary proof of U.S. citizenship for federal voter registration. It was introduced in the 119th Congress and has been a recurring priority for one wing of Congress over multiple sessions.

What does it actually do?

In plain language:

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Save Local Journalism Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Save Local Journalism Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Local journalism support legislation in the 119th Congress addressing the documented collapse of local newsrooms (~1/3 of U.S. newspapers closed since 2005; ~200 million Americans live in news deserts).

What does it actually do?

  • Local journalism tax credit — for publishers employing local journalists
  • Subscriber credit — for individual subscribers to local outlets
  • Antitrust safe harbor — limited collective-bargaining authority for news publishers vs platform aggregators
  • Public-interest journalism grants — direct funding for investigative and civic journalism
  • Library/educational use — strengthens fair-use provisions for educational distribution

Who votes when?

  • House Ways & Means + Senate Finance + House/Senate Judiciary active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #15 Power-to-the-People Council “I’m Just a Bill” pilot (Bounty Posters are themselves a form of cooperative-class civic journalism), #13 JukeBox (cooperative-class media infrastructure), and PRESS Act Bounty Poster.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: School Bus Electrification
item.description:
👀 School Bus Electrification

Federal school bus electrification 119th Congress: EPA Clean School Bus Program continuation · Tribal/rural priority · charging-infrastructure. Composes with Save-the-World Paper 2 + Universal Pre-K + School Meals. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: School Counselor Ratio Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — School Counselor Ratio

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Federal funding to lower the student-to-school-counselor ratio (currently ~400:1 average vs ASCA-recommended 250:1).

What does it actually do?

  • Title I funding stream for counselor hiring
  • Title IV Mental-Health-Services funding expansion
  • Tribal/rural priority allocations
  • Multi-tier-system-of-supports framework integration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with Save-the-World Paper 12 (Universal Childhood), #5 The Family Table, and Rural Mental Health Access Act Bounty Poster.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Section 230 Reform
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Section 230 Reform

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Section 230 reform legislation in the 119th Congress. Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act provides liability protection for online platforms regarding user-generated content. Current proposals modify the scope of protection, with significant cross-aisle disagreement on direction.

What does it actually do?

  • Algorithmic amplification carve-out — some proposals remove Section 230 protection for content algorithmically amplified by the platform
  • Bad-actor carve-out — strips protection for specific content classes (child sexual abuse material, terrorism content, deepfake non-consensual sexual content)
  • Good-Samaritan modifications — adjusts the safe-harbor for platform content moderation
  • AI-generated content provisions — addresses whether Section 230 protects AI-generated content (separate from user-generated)

Who votes when?

  • House Energy & Commerce + Judiciary + Senate Commerce + Judiciary active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

The cooperative is structurally configured to avoid the algorithmic-amplification failure mode. The cooperative does not run engagement-maximizing recommender systems. Section 230 reform affects extraction-class platforms more than cooperative-class platforms.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Section 8 Housing Voucher Reform
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Section 8 Housing Voucher Reform

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Reform of the federal Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher program in the 119th Congress, addressing voucher-acceptance discrimination, payment-standard adequacy, and administrative friction.

What does it actually do?

  • Source of income protection — federal prohibition on landlord discrimination against voucher holders
  • Payment standard — modifies HUD Fair Market Rent calculation
  • Mobility — easier inter-jurisdictional voucher transfer
  • Landlord recruitment — incentives for landlords accepting vouchers in low-poverty areas
  • Waiting list reform — addresses the documented multi-year voucher waiting lists

Who votes when?

  • House Financial Services + Senate Banking active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with Save-the-World Paper 3 (Universal Cooperative Shelter). Voucher reform composes with cooperative-housing trust architecture: voucher recipients can be cooperative members; cooperative-owned housing can accept vouchers; the cooperative composes with the federal floor.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: SNAP Modernization Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — SNAP Modernization Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Modernization of the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP / food stamps) in the 119th Congress — addressing benefit adequacy, eligibility administration, and program structure separate from the broader Farm Bill reauthorization.

What does it actually do?

  • Benefit adequacy: Updates the Thrifty Food Plan calculation methodology
  • Administrative streamlining: Reduces categorical eligibility friction across state programs
  • Work requirements: Modifies (expands or contracts depending on version) work-requirement provisions
  • Hot foods purchase: Some versions allow SNAP at prepared-foods retailers
  • Online purchasing: Expands SNAP online-purchase access

Who votes when?

  • House Agriculture + Senate Agriculture active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

SNAP composes directly with #1 Lets Make Dinner Freezer Host network and #2 Lets Get Groceries city-chapter cooperatives. Hot foods + online purchasing provisions could allow SNAP usage at cooperative-class Freezer Hosts and cooperative grocery chapters.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Social Security Disability Benefits Modernization
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Disability Benefits Modernization

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Modernization of Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) in the 119th Congress, addressing backlog, asset limits, and earned-income disincentives.

What does it actually do?

  • SSI asset limit reform — raises the long-frozen $2,000 individual / $3,000 couple asset limit
  • Application backlog — funds SSA capacity to clear documented multi-year application backlog
  • Earnings disincentives — modifies the cliff at which earned income terminates eligibility
  • Marriage penalty — addresses SSI marriage penalty
  • Plan to Achieve Self-Support — expands PASS work-incentive program

Who votes when?

  • House Ways & Means + Senate Finance active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

SSDI/SSI affect approximately 8.5 million American adults and 1 million children. Composes with #7 MSA member capital-pooling (current SSI asset limit prevents disabled members from participating in MSA at meaningful levels), #5 The Family Table disability-care coordination, and #8 Defense Klaus disability-survivor crisis services.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Social Security Fairness Act (Pub.L.118-333)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Social Security Fairness Act

Public Law 118-333 — Signed January 5, 2025

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

The Social Security Fairness Act eliminated two provisions that had reduced Social Security benefits for millions of public-sector workers since the 1980s: the Windfall Elimination Provision (WEP) and the Government Pension Offset (GPO).

It was signed into law in January 2025 with significant bipartisan support.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Stop Wall Street Looting Act / Private Equity Transparency (S.2155)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Stop Wall Street Looting Act (S.2155)

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

S.2155 in the 119th Congress would impose transparency, accountability, and limit-of-extraction provisions on private equity (PE) firms, addressing the documented pattern of PE acquisitions leading to bankruptcies in healthcare, retail, housing, and journalism.

What does it actually do?

  • PE transparency: Requires disclosure of PE ownership stakes in operating companies.
  • Joint-and-several liability: PE firms bear liability for the debts of acquired companies (closes the bankruptcy-shield loophole).
  • Healthcare PE: Specific provisions for PE-owned hospitals, nursing homes, and physician practices.
  • Worker protections: Severance and pension protections in PE-driven bankruptcies.
  • Carried-interest taxation: Closes the carried-interest tax loophole.

Who votes when?

  • Senate Banking + HELP: Active consideration
  • House companion: Pending introduction
  • President: Signature dependent on chamber action

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes directly with the cooperative-architecture thesis. Liana Banyan exists structurally as the alternative to extractive financial architectures — including PE-driven rollups in healthcare (#6 Tatiana Schlossberg Health Accords + #7 MSA), retail (#3 Lets Go Shopping cooperative marketplace), and food (#1 Lets Make Dinner + #2 Lets Get Groceries cooperative grocery model).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: SUD Treatment Access
item.description:
👀 SUD Treatment Access

Substance use disorder treatment access 119th Congress: methadone-clinic regulatory reform · MAT (medication-assisted treatment) coverage expansion · workforce provisions. Composes with Opioid Settlement Funds + Naloxone Access + Mental Health Parity. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Tribal Court Jurisdiction Expansion
item.description:
👀 Tribal Court Jurisdiction

Tribal court jurisdiction expansion 119th Congress: VAWA-2022-style criminal-jurisdiction expansion · Public Law 280 modifications · funding for Tribal courts. Composes with Native American Self-Determination + VAWA Reauthorization + Federal Sentencing Reform. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Tribal Water Rights Settlement
item.description:
👀 Tribal Water Rights Settlement

Tribal water rights settlement 119th Congress: Bureau of Reclamation funding · cross-state compact provisions · Indigenous-water-rights structural recognition. Composes with Native American Self-Determination + Water Infrastructure + IHS Modernization. 🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Universal Broadband Floor Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Universal Broadband Floor

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Universal Broadband Floor in the 119th Congress establishing minimum broadband-speed standards as a federal-policy floor for federally-funded deployment.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal minimum broadband speed standards (typically 100/20 or 100/100 Mbps)
  • Universal Service Fund (USF) reform
  • Affordability provisions composing with ACP successor
  • Tribal broadband floor

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Cooperative-class member participation in nearly every Initiative depends on functional broadband. Universal Broadband Floor is structural infrastructure for the cooperative-class economy.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Universal Charitable Deduction Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Universal Charitable Deduction

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Universal Charitable Deduction legislation in the 119th Congress restoring/expanding the above-the-line charitable deduction for non-itemizing taxpayers.

What does it actually do?

  • Above-the-line charitable deduction (no itemizing required)
  • Cap typically proposed at $300-$1,000 individual / $600-$2,000 joint
  • Applies to qualifying 501(c)(3) contributions
  • Some versions cover cooperative-class mutual-aid pool contributions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with the cooperative’s mutual-aid pools (#1 Homeless Help Solution, #5 caregiver-respite, #7 MSA emergency, #8 Defense Klaus survivor). If cooperative-class mutual-aid is qualifying-charity-eligible, contributions are tax-advantageous.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Universal Newborn Account (Baby Bonds)
item.description:
👀 Universal Newborn Account

What

Federal Baby Bonds in the 119th Congress establishing a federal-funded trust account for every newborn.

What it does

  • Initial deposit + annual deposits scaled to family income · trust matures at age 18 · educational/business/housing/cooperative-participation use only.

Composes

Save-the-World Paper 12 (Universal Childhood) · #10 VSL cooperative-finance composing.

🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Universal Pre-K Funding
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Universal Pre-K Funding

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Federal Universal Pre-Kindergarten funding legislation in the 119th Congress. Builds on existing Head Start framework with broader pre-K access provisions for 3- and 4-year-olds regardless of family income.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal grants — funds state-administered universal pre-K programs
  • Provider standards — quality and curricular standards
  • Teacher pay — addresses pre-K teacher compensation parity with K-12
  • Mixed-delivery system — funds both public-school-based and community-based pre-K
  • Indian Country provisions — Tribal pre-K funding stream

Who votes when?

  • House Education and Workforce + Senate HELP active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #4 Household Concierge (childcare connection composing), #5 The Family Table ($11T unpaid-care-work composing), and Save-the-World Paper 12 (Universal Childhood architectural reasoning). Federal pre-K floor + cooperative-class composing layer.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Universal Savings Accounts Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Universal Savings Accounts

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Universal Savings Accounts (USA) legislation in the 119th Congress proposing a tax-advantaged general-purpose savings vehicle (similar to Roth IRA but without retirement-purpose restriction).

What does it actually do?

  • Tax-advantaged general-purpose savings (no withdrawal-purpose restriction)
  • Annual contribution limits
  • Income-eligibility provisions
  • Substitution-routing compatibility (some versions allow USA → cooperative-class participation)

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with Cooperative Finance Trio (#7 + #10 + #11). USA vehicle could compose with MSA member capital pooling for tax-advantaged cooperative-class participation if Substitution-routing provisions are included.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Universal School Meals Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Universal School Meals

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Universal School Meals legislation in the 119th Congress, building on the pandemic-era universal school meals waiver (which ended in 2022) and several states’ subsequent universal-school-meals laws.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal universal-eligibility school breakfast and lunch
  • Eliminates application/eligibility-verification process
  • Expands Community Eligibility Provision (CEP) thresholds
  • Funds summer-meals continuity programs
  • Tribal Schools coverage provisions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #1 Lets Make Dinner (cooperative-class food infrastructure for school-age kids) and Save-the-World Paper 12 (Universal Childhood). Universal school meals is the federal floor; cooperative-class composing layer fills the gaps (weekend, summer, and household-level food security).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: VAWA Reauthorization 2025
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — VAWA Reauthorization 2025

Violence Against Women Act Reauthorization

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

The Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) was first enacted in 1994 and is reauthorized periodically. The 2025 reauthorization in the 119th Congress continues the program and modifies specific provisions.

What does it actually do?

In plain language:

  • Reauthorizes federal grant programs supporting domestic-violence shelters, rape crisis centers, and survivor-services nonprofits.
  • Maintains the Office on Violence Against Women within DOJ and its grant-making functions.
  • Updates specific provisions including survivor housing protections, economic-empowerment supports, and Tribal court jurisdiction.
  • Funding levels subject to annual appropriations even after reauthorization.

Who votes when?

  • House and Senate: Active consideration in 119th Congress
  • Historical pattern: VAWA has historically passed with bipartisan support though with periodic controversies over specific provisions
  • President: Signature expected if both chambers pass

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

VAWA-funded shelters and services are part of the safety-net infrastructure that the cooperative’s Initiative #8 Defense Klaus (“For Someone You Love” — Robert Kaiser First Shield UK + Ruth Glenn US Shield) is structurally designed to compose with, not replace.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Veteran Crisis Line Expansion Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Veteran Crisis Line Expansion Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Expansion of the Veterans Crisis Line program in the 119th Congress, addressing capacity, response time, and integration with the broader 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline.

What does it actually do?

  • Staffing: Increases counselor staffing for the Veterans Crisis Line (Press 1 from 988).
  • Wait time: Sets statutory maximum wait time for veteran callers.
  • Follow-up: Funds structured follow-up protocols for veterans who reach crisis services.
  • Peer-support: Expands peer-support specialist availability (veterans helping veterans).
  • Family services: Adds family-member-of-veteran callers to qualifying-caller categories.

Who votes when?

  • House and Senate Veterans’ Affairs Committees active consideration.

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with the cooperative’s veteran-Crown anchors on Initiative #15 (Mark Wahlberg, John Cena, Gary Sinise — the Lt. Dan Band founder, Sam Elliott, Tom Hanks). Crisis Line capacity directly affects veteran members of the cooperative.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Veterans Auto and Education Improvement Act (H.R.1289)
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Veterans Auto and Education Improvement Act (H.R.1289)

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

H.R.1289 in the 119th Congress would expand veteran education and transportation benefits, addressing gaps in the Post-9/11 GI Bill and VA-administered transportation assistance programs.

What does it actually do?

  • Education benefits: Extends Post-9/11 GI Bill eligibility windows and expands covered programs (apprenticeships, on-the-job training).
  • Transportation: Improves VA transportation assistance for veterans accessing care, especially in rural areas.
  • Spousal benefits: Extends spouse-and-dependent benefit transferability.
  • Workforce reentry: Funds veteran-specific apprenticeship programs.

Who votes when?

  • House Veterans’ Affairs Committee: Active markup
  • Senate Veterans’ Affairs: Companion under review
  • President: Signature historically routine for veteran-benefit legislation

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with the cooperative’s veteran-Crown anchors on Initiative #15 (Mark Wahlberg, John Cena, Gary Sinise, Sam Elliott, Tom Hanks — all veterans-supporters) and with Initiative #16 Brass Tacks (apprenticeship-pipeline composition with Mike Rowe Council Crown).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Veterans Homelessness Prevention Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Veterans Homelessness Prevention

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Veterans homelessness prevention legislation in the 119th Congress, building on HUD-VASH (HUD Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing) and SSVF (Supportive Services for Veteran Families) programs.

What does it actually do?

  • HUD-VASH voucher expansion
  • SSVF funding stabilization
  • Veteran-specific rapid-rehousing capacity
  • Prevention-side intervention for at-risk veterans
  • Family-of-veteran inclusive provisions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #1 Lets Make Dinner Homeless Help Solution, Veteran Crisis Line Expansion Bounty Poster, Veterans Auto-Education Improvement Bounty Poster, and the cooperative’s veteran-Crown Council anchors (Wahlberg, Cena, Sinise, Sam Elliott, Hanks, The Rock).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Water Infrastructure Investment Act
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Water Infrastructure Investment Act

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Water infrastructure legislation in the 119th Congress addressing lead-pipe replacement, PFAS contamination remediation, and rural water-system viability.

What does it actually do?

  • Lead service line replacement — full federal funding for the documented ~9 million U.S. lead service lines
  • PFAS remediation — funds municipal water-system PFAS removal
  • Rural water systems — operating-grant support for small water systems
  • Drought response — funds drinking-water resilience in arid regions
  • Tribal water access — separate funding stream for Tribal water infrastructure

Who votes when?

  • House Transportation & Infrastructure + Senate Environment & Public Works active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with Save-the-World Paper 5 (Universal Earth Stewardship) and Save-the-World Paper 3 (Universal Cooperative Shelter — water infrastructure is structurally part of housing). Lead-pipe replacement directly affects cooperative-class member housing health.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Whistleblower Protection Expansion
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Whistleblower Protection Expansion

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Expansion of federal whistleblower protections in the 119th Congress, addressing gaps in current law for federal employees, contractors, and private-sector whistleblowers reporting fraud, waste, or abuse.

What does it actually do?

  • Federal employee protections — strengthens Merit Systems Protection Board enforcement
  • Contractor protections — covers federal contractor employees disclosing fraud
  • Anti-retaliation — increases damages and remedies for retaliation
  • Reporting channels — expands acceptable disclosure channels
  • Intelligence-community — limited expansion of IC whistleblower channels

Who votes when?

  • House Oversight + Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with the Federal-Congressional Body Cam transparency doctrine and the cooperative’s broader structural commitment to “accountability is dignity, not vulnerability.” Whistleblower protections are infrastructure for the transparency the cooperative argues for.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Worker Misclassification Reform
item.description:
👀 Worker Misclassification Reform

What

Federal worker misclassification reform in the 119th Congress (ABC test standardization, gig-economy worker classification).

What it does

  • Federal ABC-test standardization · gig-platform classification standards · cooperative-class member classification clarity · enforcement-budget expansion.

Composes

#16 Brass Tacks worker-as-member structural distinction · NLRB Open Letter.

🟡 Provisional. Help Each Other Help Ourselves.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Worker-Cooperative Conversion Tax Deferral
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Worker-Cooperative Conversion Tax Deferral

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Federal tax-deferral legislation in the 119th Congress facilitating retiring small-business owners’ sale of business to a worker-cooperative-class structure (Section 1042-style deferral expanded to ESOP-cousin worker-cooperative conversions).

What does it actually do?

  • Tax-deferred capital-gains treatment for sellers transferring to qualifying worker cooperatives
  • Holding-period requirements aligned with ESOP analog provisions
  • SBA loan-guarantee programs for worker-cooperative buyout financing
  • Technical assistance funding for conversion process

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Direct composing with the cooperative’s federation goal (Step 5 — Federate It). Worker-cooperative conversions of existing businesses can adopt the LBCCL framework and federate with Liana Banyan’s cooperative-class economy. Composes with #7 MSA, #10 VSL, #11 Lets Make Bread for conversion-financing capital pooling.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Workforce Housing Tax Credit
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Workforce Housing Tax Credit

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Workforce Housing Tax Credit in the 119th Congress addressing the “missing middle” housing affordability gap (households earning 80-120% of area median income who don’t qualify for LIHTC but cannot afford market-rate housing).

What does it actually do?

  • Federal tax credit for missing-middle housing development
  • Set-asides for cooperative-housing trust projects
  • Composes with LIHTC framework
  • State allocation provisions

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with Save-the-World Paper 3 (Universal Cooperative Shelter), Affordable Housing Tax Credit Act Bounty Poster (LIHTC), and the cooperative-housing trust architecture.

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act Reauthorization
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — WIOA Reauthorization

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Reauthorization of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) in the 119th Congress. WIOA is the major federal workforce-development law funding apprenticeships, job training, and dislocated-worker services through state and local workforce boards.

What does it actually do?

  • Apprenticeship expansion — funds Registered Apprenticeships and Apprenticeship Hubs
  • Workforce board governance — modifies state/local workforce-board structure
  • Dislocated workers — extends services for trade-affected and displaced workers
  • Youth services — programs for 16-24 year-olds entering the workforce
  • Adult education — basic-skills and English-language programs

Who votes when?

  • House Education and Workforce + Senate HELP active consideration; historically bipartisan

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes with #16 Brass Tacks (apprenticeship pipeline for Step 4 Node network), #12 Harper Guild (LB-CCL credentialing federation with WIOA-funded programs), and Mike Rowe Council seat (mikeroweWORKS scholarship-pipeline composition).
